# MCT oracle vectors

`vectors.json` holds bit-exact input/output vectors for the multi-component
transforms, generated from the OpenJPEG C reference (`mct.c`, `invert.c`) so the
Go port in `internal/mct` can be validated bit-for-bit.

All `float32` values are stored as their raw IEEE-754 bit pattern, encoded as a
decimal `uint32`, so comparison is exact (NaN-safe). Integer values are stored
as plain JSON integers.

## Structure

- `rct` ‚Äî reversible color transform (`opj_mct_encode` / `opj_mct_decode`),
  int32. `n` samples; `c0/c1/c2` are the inputs, `enc_*` the forward-transform
  outputs, `dec_*` the inverse-transform outputs (inverse applied to the same
  `c0/c1/c2` inputs, not to `enc_*`).
- `ict` ‚Äî irreversible color transform (`opj_mct_encode_real` /
  `opj_mct_decode_real`), float32 bits. Same layout as `rct`.
- `custom` ‚Äî arbitrary NxN matrix transform, one object per case
  (`nbcomp` = 3..6):
  - `matrix` ‚Äî `nbcomp*nbcomp` float32 bits, row-major.
  - `enc_in` / `enc_out` ‚Äî `opj_mct_encode_custom`, int32 component data
    (`nbcomp` arrays of `n` samples). The matrix is applied in 1<<13 fixed
    point via `opj_int_fix_mul`.
  - `dec_in` / `dec_out` ‚Äî `opj_mct_decode_custom`, float32-bits component data.
    The matrix is applied directly in float32.
- `inversion` ‚Äî `opj_matrix_inversion_f`, one object per case:
  - `in` ‚Äî source matrix, `nbcomp*nbcomp` float32 bits, row-major.
  - `ok` ‚Äî whether the C inversion succeeded (false for the singular case).
  - `out` ‚Äî inverse matrix float32 bits (meaningful only when `ok`).

The last inversion case has a zeroed first column, so the C pivot search fails
and `ok` is false ‚Äî this exercises the singular-matrix error path.

## Regeneration

The harness source is `oracle/harness/w4/mct_gen.c` (gitignored). From the repo
root:

```
gcc -O2 -I oracle/openjpeg/src/lib/openjp2 -I oracle/openjpeg/build/src/lib/openjp2 \
    oracle/harness/w4/mct_gen.c oracle/openjpeg/build/bin/libopenjp2.a -lm -lpthread \
    -o /tmp/mct_gen
/tmp/mct_gen > testdata/vectors/mct/vectors.json
```

The harness uses a fixed-seed xorshift64 PRNG, so output is deterministic.

## `ict.dec_c1` ‚Äî shipped-binary (shared-library) provenance

Every array in this file is generated by linking the static `libopenjp2.a`
EXCEPT `ict.dec_c1` (the green output of `opj_mct_decode_real`), which is taken
from a build linked against the shipped shared library `libopenjp2.so` ‚Äî the
library `opj_decompress` (our decode parity target) actually loads at runtime.

Why the split: OpenJPEG is built `-O3 -ffast-math`. `-ffast-math` enables
`-fassociative-math`, so gcc reassociates the green computation
`(y - u*0.34413) - v*0.71414` into `y - (u*0.34413 + v*0.71414)` in the SSE
code it emits for the shared library. That reassociation changes the result by
1 ULP on some samples, which after round-to-nearest-even + DC level shift flips
the final integer on a handful of green pixels (e.g. 4/2.07M on `_00042.j2k`,
73/3.19M on `issue135.j2k`). `internal/mct.DecodeReal` reproduces the shipped
`y - (u*0.34413 + v*0.71414)` form so whole-image decodes are bit-exact with
`opj_decompress`; `ict.dec_c1` is regenerated to match. `dec_c0`/`dec_c2` (red,
blue) are single multiply-adds with no reassociation freedom, so they are
identical under either build and need no regeneration.

The forward (`enc_*`), `custom`, and `inversion` vectors are still linked from
`libopenjp2.a` and validate the Go forward/custom/inversion paths as written
(source order). Those C paths are ALSO reassociated by `-ffast-math` in the
shipped `.so`, so if/when an encode gate requires byte-parity with
`opj_compress`, the forward MCT (and `invert.c`) will need the same treatment ‚Äî
flagged as an encode-side (W9) follow-up, out of scope for the decode gate.

To reproduce `ict.dec_c1`, build the same harness against the shared library and
copy that one array over:

```
gcc -O2 -I oracle/openjpeg/src/lib/openjp2 -I oracle/openjpeg/build/src/lib/openjp2 \
    oracle/harness/w4/mct_gen.c -L oracle/openjpeg/build/bin -lopenjp2 \
    -Wl,-rpath,$PWD/oracle/openjpeg/build/bin -lm -lpthread -o /tmp/mct_gen_so
/tmp/mct_gen_so   # take its ict.dec_c1 array
```
